entry.title: SAP Sales Cloud v1 and v2 now support Microsoft Graph API with Server Side Groupware Integration
entry.content:
For those unfamiliar, Server Side Groupware Integration allows for a seamless, behind-the-scenes synchronization of your emails, calendar events, and contacts between your CRM (SAP Sales Cloud) and your email and collaboration platform (Microsoft 365). Traditionally, this might have relied on client-side plugins or Exchange Web Services, that will be deprecated later next year.

What does this mean for you?

The adoption of the Microsoft Graph API brings several key advantages:

  • Enhanced Reliability: The Microsoft Graph API is a modern, RESTful API known for its stability and performance. This translates to a more dependable synchronization of your critical sales-related communications and activities.
  • Improved Security: Leveraging the Microsoft Graph API aligns with modern security standards, ensuring a more secure connection between your SAP Sales Cloud and Microsoft 365 environments.
  • Future-Proofing: By utilizing the Microsoft Graph API, SAP Sales Cloud is embracing a forward-looking integration approach, ensuring compatibility with future updates and features from Microsoft.
  • Simplified Configuration: Server-side integration often simplifies the setup and maintenance process for administrators compared to client-side solutions.

Key Benefits of Server Side Groupware Integration:

  • Automatic Synchronization: Emails received in Outlook can be automatically linked to relevant accounts, contacts, and opportunities within SAP Sales Cloud.
  • Calendar Integration: Keep your schedule aligned! Appointments created in either SAP Sales Cloud or Outlook can seamlessly sync, providing a unified view of your commitments.
  • Contact Synchronization: Ensure your contact information is consistent across both platforms, reducing the risk of outdated or duplicate entries.

What versions are included?

This exciting update applies to both:

  • SAP Sales Cloud v1 (formerly SAP Cloud for Customer - C4C)
  • SAP Sales Cloud v2

This means that regardless of which SAP Sales Cloud version your organization utilizes, you can now benefit from this enhanced integration with Microsoft 365.

In Conclusion:

The support for Microsoft Graph API in SAP Sales Cloud v1 and v2's Server Side Groupware Integration marks a significant step forward in providing a more integrated and efficient sales experience. By leveraging the power and reliability of the Microsoft Graph API, SAP is empowering sales teams to focus on what matters most – building relationships and closing deals – with the confidence that their communication and scheduling are seamlessly connected.

What are your thoughts on this new integration? Share your experiences and questions in the comments below!

entry.title: Accelerate Every Stage of Your SAP Transformation with Enterprise Automation from UiPath and SAP
entry.content:
SAP customers are under pressure to move faster—modernize core systems, accelerate your S/4HANA journey, adopt AI, and do it all while maintaining stability across your operations. That’s why SAP has made enterprise automation a strategic priority across SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

At the center of this strategy is SAP’s Solution Extension partnership with UiPath, the recognized leader in both Robotic Process Automation (RPA) and Intelligent Document Processing (IDP).

By embedding UiPath’s market-leading automation technology directly into SAP Build, SAP delivers a natively integrated solution that empowers you to:

  • Automate across your entire IT landscape, including SAP and non-SAP systems
  • Extend your ERP with automation instead of custom code, maintaining a clean core before, during, and after migration
  • Accelerate your S/4HANA migration by using automation at every phase
  • Include any document in your workflows using enterprise-grade IDP
  • Scale automation securely with built-in governance and native BTP integration

With the UiPath and SAP joint solution, you gain a powerful foundation for end-to-end automation—no matter how complex your systems or processes. From business processes that cut across SAP and non-SAP systems alike—connecting data, decisions, and actions across ERPs, CRMs, emails, documents, and more—UiPath and SAP let you level up to a holistic approach to automation that drives transformational outcomes and AI readiness.

This new eBook from ASUG and UiPath explores how automation helps SAP customers move faster, stay flexible, and prepare for the future with confidence.

In this eBook, you’ll learn:

  • How automation supports every stage of your SAP transformation—from ECC to S/4HANA and beyond
  • How SAP Build and UiPath work together on BTP to reduce complexity, increase agility, and deliver long-term value
  • Why automation is essential for maintaining a clean core, enabling composable architecture, and scaling AI across your business

💡See how SAP is putting automation to work

Hi @Kamil14 

You can get these details by using SAP standard reports.

Execute T Code - FBL3N

  • Input Company Code

entry.title: Re: ItemID with automatic generation vs. item connector
entry.content: That resource is correct, the item connector disregards the item auto-generate ID setting.  There is no way to auto-populate an itemID and the file requires an entry as required and will use any entry you provide it.  Regardless of this setting the item ID behaves normally - if an itemID exists it will try to update depending on your other fields and if it doesn't it will add the item.

entry.title: Re: Setting the GeoMap information in a model
entry.content:
Hello @adriano1973 

Maybe I don't get you right, but you need Geo Information (latitude and longitude) only in the SAC model OR the SAC dimension, but not in both.

Earlier, in the old Account Model you had to model the Geo data as key figures.

But in the new model it can be 2 special dimension properties, where the data can be loaded directly into the dimension.

Upon loading a so-called Geo_Point dimension is created, which concatenates lat. and long.

Then you use these properties in the geo map widget:

So you don't have to link or associate the dimension properties to any dimensions in your model.

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: Using Association in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello @BenedictV 

associations in a Datasphere view, especially a FACT view support only the Analytic Model by enabling attributes for master data (dimension view associated) or the display options of "description" only (instead of key only), "key and description" or "description and key" in e.g. the SAC story, which consumes the Analytic Model. This is like the "current truth" of master data attributes

If you want to directly have some master data in your data records which remain stable for historic truth, then you better join these attributes to your fact data.

See also this Blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/slowly-changing-dimensions-in-sap-data-warehouse-analytics-cloud-done-right/ba-p/13549370 

BR, Martin

entry.title: RFM/RFE Modelling with SAP CDP: Basic, Advanced and Custom
entry.content:
Understanding customer behavior is  key to personalized marketing — and RFM/RFE modeling helps you do exactly that in SAP Customer Data Platform.

RFM modeling is a standard marketing analysis technique used to segment customers based on their purchasing behavior. It helps businesses understand and predict customer value by looking at three key factors:

  • Recency (R):
    How recently has the customer made a purchase?
    Customers who purchased recently are more likely to respond to promotions than those who haven’t purchased in a while.

  • Frequency (F):
    How often does the customer make purchases?
    Frequent customers are typically more engaged and valuable.

  • Monetary value (M):
    How much money has the customer spent?
    High spenders are usually more profitable and potentially more loyal.

Every factor is scored individually for every customer, typically on a scale from 1 (worst) to 5 (best). 
Then a total sum of all three factors can be made giving every customer a score of in between 3 and 15. This then enables targeted and personalized marketing with better marketing ROI.

Some business models are less about purchases but more about engagement. This variation is then called RFE modelling, with E for Engagement.

SAP Customer Data Platform provides RFM/RFE analysis out of the box with an intuitive wizard. 

Let me show you how to use it for basic, advanced or custom use cases.

Basic

RFM Wizard - Basic

For most use cases it's very simple. As you can see here you just need to create an RFM indicator, choose the time frame you want to have evaluated, basic setup (default) and save. That's it!

Advanced

RFM Wizard - Advanced

In the basic setup recency, frequency and monetary value will have equal weight. If you want to change the relative weights you can do so in the advanced setup. As you can see here for example giving 50% to the monetary value and only 25% to recency and frequency each.

RFM will evaluate SAP CDP's out-of-the-box order activity. How to use a different activity I will show you in the custom section.

 

For RFE it's the same with the only difference that you can choose an activity which represents the engagement.

RFE Wizard

Both RFM and RFE work for profiles and groups, e.g. accounts.

 

Custom

Now there may be use cases for which you want to give more specific instructions on how to segment your customers by different factors into several buckets or tiers. For example if you're using other activities then the default order activity, like if you're tracking online and offline purchases in different activities. Or if you want to limit the evaluation time of certain factors like monetary value (SAP's default: all time).

Let me show you how to do this.

First for every factor you need to create an activity indicator that calculates it. Take a look at how recency would be calculated individually, but for the last 5 years (5x365 days = 1825 days):

Custom Recency Activity Indicator

Then with the help of our new generic percent ranking feature you can create a custom indicator for profiles or groups which evenly distributes all profiles or groups into the number of percentile buckets that you specify, 5 in below example (meaning 20% per bucket):

Custom 5 Buckets for Recency

This results in recency score from 1 to 5 just like the standard CDP RFM model does it, but with this custom approach you can choose other activities, choose a different time frame, choose other factors. The possibilities are endless! Think of evaluating return frequency or amount to identify customers who order a lot but also return a lot, thus judging how profitable your customers really are.

Once you have created all activity indicators for all factors relevant to your business model together with the generic percentile ranking custom indicators you can create one final custom indicator which simply adds them together, like CDP RFM in standard does it. Here you can also apply weight factors if you want to. Like in this example (not related to RFM modelling but still showing how to do the math):

Weight Factors

In B2B business models you can aggregate activities from contacts up to accounts and even up through the account hierarchy with the help of child indicators. Here as well you can apply the generic percentile ranking for targeted segmentation. But that's probably a topic for a separate blog.

 

Conclusion

SAP Customer Data Platform seamlessly brings together front-office and back-office data so that customer engagements become meaningful and in the back-office business decisions can be made, powered by contextual customer insights. 

With SAP CDP’s flexible RFM and RFE capabilities, businesses gain a 360° view of customer behavior. Whether you're targeting recent high spenders or loyal engagers, these insights help drive targeted action with measurable impact.

Let us know what cool use cases you're implementing with the help of SAP Customer Data Platform!

 

More Information

For further information refer to SAP Customer Data Platform or to SAP Customer Data Platform | SAP Help Portal.

In detail refer to 

  • Creating an RFM Indicator
  • Creating an RFE Indicator
  • Generic Percentile Ranking 
  • Creating a Child Indicator

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP mock REST service
entry.content:
Local testing can for instance be done by:

  • Providing test data for remote service in srv/external/data.
  • Providing a handler for remote service to allow simulating functions and actions.

The provided data and handler for remote service is only loaded when running in development mode where no credentials are specified.

For convenience (separation) the mocked, remote service can be launched in separate terminal via cds mock <ext serv> --port <xyz>.

After, in another terminal, the normal cds watch command can be executed which will now run against the mocked service.

 

It may be needed to clean up the file .cds-services.json located in the user directory. On windows that would be in c:\Users\<user>\.

entry.title: LeanIX Fact Sheet types and subtypes - Quick reference guide
entry.content:
SAP LeanIX is a powerful Enterprise Architecture Management tool. To get the most value out of it, we create a digital twin of an enterprise using various fact sheets. SAP LeanIX provides twelve key fact sheet types across four areas. We need to create at least a set of fact sheets to gain a reasonably good understanding of the enterprise we are trying to model.

The first steps to model an enterprise in SAP LeanIX can be frustrating. It's working on a Sudoku puzzle where there are not enough 'pre-defined' numbers in all nine boxes. It leads to increased uncertainty and fewer logical deductions to solve the puzzle. Similarly, in SAP LeanIX, we need to assemble a certain number of fact sheets in each domain and link them logically to build a reasonably good model for initial discussions with fellow architects and the project team members. We also need to tag fact sheets with subtype and build a logical and intuitive hierarchy that is easy to understand with minimal/no explanation.

Whenever I work in LeanIX, I refer to the Fact Sheet Modeling Guidelines to ensure that I am assigning the right fact sheet types, subtypes and building a logical hierarchy.  I also created the following diagram to refer to the Fact sheet types, subtypes and hierarchy. I hope this helps the EA community as a quick reference guide.

SAP LeanIX features and its guidelines will evolve (as time goes by 😀). Please refer to the latest guidelines from SAP LeanIX.

I look forward to your comments on how you are using fact sheet types, subtypes and hierarchy. What decisions and insights are you deriving from modelling an enterprise in SAP LeanIX?

entry.title: Template Allocation and Splitting Structure
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

My client requires indirect costs to be distributed based on the Process Order Quantity. To meet this requirement, I created a template (environment 009) using the formula “Order Total Quantity” for both plan and actual allocations.

During the cost estimate of the process order, the planned cost is calculated as expected, and actual costs are being derived correctly after executing CPTD, based on the formula: Plan Rate × Order Quantity.

For actual activity cost calculation, we have maintained a splitting structure with Rule 12 – Actual Activity Quantity for all activity types, including the indirect ones used in the template. However, when running KSS2, the amounts posted to the GL accounts associated with the indirect activities (defined in the template) are being distributed equally across all activity types within the cost center.

Could you please confirm whether Rule 12 is appropriate for these indirect activity types that rely on template allocation using “Order Total Quantity”? Or should an alternative splitting rule be maintained? Also, is there any configuration step I might have overlooked?

Please note that the splitting structure has already been assigned to the relevant cost centers.

Thank you in advance.

Best regards,
Merin

entry.title: Re: Template Allocation
entry.content:
Hi Vijaykumar,
By creation of a copy template with environment 001 (cost estimate) the issue has been resolved and now the indirect costs are coming in CK11N. 
Through mapping of Costing Sheet to Plan and Actual Costing Variant of Process Order, in Order Cost details also indirect costs are properly getting calculated.

Thank you for the response.

Regards

entry.title: Introducing SAPUI5 Version Preview in SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
SAPUI5 is a cornerstone of SAP's user interface technology, providing a robust framework for building responsive and enterprise-ready web applications. Regular updates to SAPUI5 bring innovative features and critical bug fixes, ensuring that applications remain modern and efficient. Until now, the latest SAPUI5 versions were automatically adopted in production environments, which limited opportunities for early evaluation ahead of deployment.

Recognizing the need for controlled testing of SAPUI5 updates, we have introduced a new feature that allows administrators to

preview upcoming SAPUI5 versions in non-productive environments within SAP Build Work Zone. This capability was developed in response to customer feedback, aiming to provide a controlled environment for evaluating SAPUI5 updates in SAP Build Work Zone, thereby avoiding immediate changes in production environments.

What's New: SAPUI5 Version Preview Feature in SAP Build Work Zone

The new "Preview SAPUI5 Latest Version" feature empowers administrators to test upcoming SAPUI5 versions in designated non-productive environments within SAP Build Work Zone. This proactive approach allows customers to explore upcoming updates in a controlled setting, supporting a smoother and more predictable transition to production environments.

This feature is available in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced and standard edition and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone.

Key Benefits:

This feature offers several advantages. Administrators can gain early access to the Release to Customer (RTC) version of SAPUI5. They have two weeks before its general availability, allowing ample time for testing. By enabling the preview setting on specific non-productive sites, they can assess compatibility and performance with the upcoming version. This controlled testing environment helps identify and address potential issues, reducing the likelihood of disruptions in production. After the two-week preview period, the RTC version becomes the default, aligning all sites with the latest stable release.

How it works – step by step

1. Enable Preview:

In the site editor, administrators can activate the "Preview SAPUI5 Latest Version" setting for selected non-productive sites.

Toggle turned off in site settings

Toggle turned on in site settings

Warning message while saving the changes 

Site preview on and further information

Site directory with a site where preview is turned on

2. Testing Period

The designated sites will consume the RTC version of SAPUI5 for a two-week period, during which thorough testing and validation can be conducted.

To enable this in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, customers can create a dedicated site in their existing PROD or PRE-PROD sub-accounts (or QA, if preferred), there is no need to create a new sub-account. The relevant content can be transported to this test site, where the preview option for SAPUI5 can then be enabled. In SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, customer must setup a new sub-account (pre-prod) to use the UI5 preview feature.

3. Adjustment Option

If needed during the preview period, administrators can turn off the preview setting to revert the site back to the previously active version.

4. Automatic Update

After the two-week period, the version for all sites, ensuring consistency across environments.

SAPUI5 versions

SAPUI5 provides new versions regularly to deliver innovative features and also to provide patches with bug fixes. See What's New in SAPUI5 and the SAPUI5 What's New Viewer to learn about the new features and Versioning and Maintenance of SAPUI5 to understand the versioning concept. A new version is consumed by default by SAP BTP services like SAP Build Work Zone to have a solid basis for new capabilities and an up-to-date user experience.

Approximately 2 weeks before a new version is consumed by SAP cloud solutions, it is released to customers (RTC). In this timeframe you may now test a new version with SAP Build Work Zone. Learn about the planned SAPUI5 versions with their planned RTC date in SAP Note 3504933.

Learn More! 

Important references:

  • See the planned SAPUI5 versions with their planned release date in SAP Note 3504933.
  • See the released SAPUI5 versions in SAPUI5 Versions Maintenance Status.

For more insights into SAPUI5 and SAP Build Work Zone:

  • Explore the SAPUI5: What's New and When Is It Coming? blog post on the SAP Community
  • SAP Build Work Zone on: sap.com / SAP Community
  • SAP Application Development and Automation Community Group

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Referenced UI5 Versions and Release Schedule
entry.content:
SAP BTP ABAP Environment (aka "Steampunk") relies on SAP Fiori as the supported UI technology and design system. One of the key elements of SAP Fiori is the SAP UI5 library. Regarding SAP BTP ABAP Environment, the SAP UI5 library is relevant for:

  • The ABAP Fiori Launchpad, 
  • The delivered SAP apps,
  • The custom apps which are built with SAP Fiori Elements, 
  • The custom apps which are built with freestyle SAP UI5 library.

SAP UI5 versions do have their own release cycle. The referenced SAP UI5 version is tightly coupled with the SAP BTP ABAP Environment release. It is not possible to configure other SAP UI5 versions due to functional dependencies between front-end features like in the ABAP Fiori Launchpad and in the backend.

 

Release Schedule 

SAP BTP ABAP Environment is delivered on a quarterly basis (see planned maintenance). SAP UI5 is delivered on a monthly basis. Therefore, each third SAP UI5 major version is consumed by a SAP BTP ABAP Environment release.

Corrections of SAP UI5 versions are delivered via patch versions. The reference to a patched SAP UI5 version can be updated via a hotfix collection of the SAP BTP ABAP Environment (see planned maintenance above).

The following table shows the release cycle of the SAP BTP ABAP Environment releases with the referenced used SAP UI5 version.

SAP BTP ABAP Environment releaseSAP BTP ABAP Environment hotfix collectionReferenced SAP UI5 version
2505 1.133.x
 HFC 1 (pre-upgrade)1.133.1
 HFC 2 (pre-upgrade)1.133.1
 HFC 3 (standard upgrade)1.133.2
 HFC 41.133.4 (current)
2508 1.136.x (planned)
2511 1.139.x (planned)

The currently referenced SAP UI5 version is shown in the ABAP Fiori Launchpad of the SAP BTP ABAP Environment. Please open the user account menu in the right corner and choose About.

entry.title: Re: File contains element <Nm> that is not defined in the...
entry.content:
The issue is similar to the symptom mentioned in the SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3400255 

Check the <Nm> tag missing under this tree <RltdAgts >-<DbtrAgt >-<FinInstnId>-<BIC>-<Nm>-<PstlAdr >.  

standard mapping does not have all the entries that correspond to the standard CGI format because they are not widely used . 

you can create an own mapping by copying the standard delivered format and add this tag at the mentioned location in hierarchy and the file will pass through . 

How to Create a Format Mapping | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Master data mass maintenance in SAP S/4HANA Public cloud.
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Mass Maintenance is one of the most significant features that helps users perform bulk data activities in a simple and efficient manner. This functionality can be used to manage changes in both Product and Business Partner master data.

In this blog, I will demonstrate how to perform a simple change in Product Master Data using the mass maintenance process. The same concept applies to Business Partner Master Data as well.

In this blog post, I'll walk you through a real-world scenario where we need to reassign the Purchasing Group for a large set of materials and demonstrate how to handle this using Export, Update, and Mass Upload functionality.

Business Scenario:

A company maintains thousands of materials, each currently assigned to some Purchasing Group ex.001. Due to internal realignment, a portion of these materials must now be reassigned to Purchasing Groups 001, 002 and 003.
Instead of updating each material one by one, we use a mass upload process that includes the following activates:

Applications Involved:
• Export Master Data – Products
• Manage Mass Maintenance – Products
• Start Mass Maintenance – Products
• Track Mass Changes – Products

Steps to Perform:

I. Export Existing Material Data
Use the Export Master Data – Products app:
• Select the Purchasing Group field (or any other fields to be changed).
• Apply selection criteria to filter the relevant materials.

• Export the list to Excel. This file contains the current system values.
Now, edit the Excel file to replace the existing purchasing group values with the new ones (e.g., change from 001 to 002 or 003 as applicable). Save the updated file.

_______________________________________________________________________________________
II. Create a Mass Change Process
Use the Manage Mass Maintenance – Products app. This acts as a dashboard showing all mass change processes (active, in progress, or completed).

From here:
• Click "Create Process" or open the Start Mass Maintenance – Products app directly.
• Enter a meaningful Process Description.
• Upload the updated Excel file.

• Click Start. The system will detect and display the fields in scope based on the uploaded template.
______________________________________________________________________________________
III. Mass Change Process – 4 Key Stages
The mass maintenance process has four core steps:
🔍1. Scope

• The system reads the uploaded file and identifies which fields are in scope.
• It validates whether the fields are available and editable.

✏️2. Edit

• You can review and optionally modify the uploaded values.
• The system validates all field values in the material master, not just the one being updated.
• This may result in warnings for fields like material descriptions, units of measure, etc., even if they're not part of your update.
✅Example: If a material description exceeds allowed length, a warning may appear, but it won’t block purchasing group changes.
• Review warnings. If they're unrelated to your updated field, proceed.

✔️3. Validation

• The system checks whether:
> New values are valid per configuration.
> Only the changed values will be processed.
• If needed, you can roll back to previous steps.

✅4. Activation

• This is the final upload step where changes are committed to the system.
• After successful activation, the new purchasing group values are reflected in the material master.
You can view the results by:
• Clicking "Show All changes" or using the Track Mass Changes – Products app for a detailed change log.
_______________________________________________________________________________________
🧠 Key Tips
• Always run the Export Master Data to ensure you’re working with up-to-date values.
• Don’t worry about unrelated warnings unless they block the update.
• Use the Rollback option if needed before final activation.
• Upload templates must match system field format and value types.
_______________________________________________________________________________________
✅Summary
The mass change process in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud offers a safe, controlled, and efficient way to manage large-scale data changes without custom development. Whether you're updating purchasing groups, MRP types, or other fields the mass upload method ensures:
• Better accuracy,
• Consistent governance,
• And minimal manual effort.

entry.title: Creation of Deep Links in SuccessFactors Suites
entry.content:
📢What is Deep Link ?  📢 It is a hyperlink that helps the system users / those newly experiencing the system for the first time to navigate to the precise target location of user Information/company Information out of the crowded system details, so you will not be lost. You can place this link anywhere on your company website / Emails / external systems by clicking on it will drive you to the desired location, even in the SuccessFactors home page (tiles/cards) or in the main navigation menu.

🗣🔊What does 1H-2025 say on the Deep link for those who opted (PPX UI Experience) - SAP recommend that to configure deep links to cards or categories and stop using legacy block deep links in your system. Currently, only certain block deep links containing block types can be resolved for the latest People Profile.

PP3 UI (Legacy People Profile Experience)

PPX UI (Latest People Profile)

BLOCKS

Categories

Portlets

Summary Cards

 

👨‍🏫What are all the Data elements that comprise forming complete Deep links? 

  1. Domain Name - Name used by the application server, you can make use of this information from the browser URL, (Tip – Make use of data before “/”), and it varies by customer and region (https://hcm-eu10-sales.hr.cloud.sap)

 

  1. Deep link Path 🛣 – True deep links begin with “/sf/” and deep link paths are the first filter and navigation definition of the data/location, where the user would like to navigate to. Deep link would vary based on the location/destination/sf-module of the data where we need to land the user. Currently, we are exploring the employee profile information, so our Deep link path would be “sf/liveprofile”.

Tip 💡 – We can’t define the Deep link path on our own, it was pre-defined by the SAP for the different modules and based on the nature of data where the deep link is supported, please find the link for more Deep Link path on your business requirement SAP Help Portal - Deep Links

 

  1. Parameters – After Deep Link path, parameters which help for the fine-granular navigation refinement with the help of data (i.e. Key value pair) which directs the user to the destination page where we are expecting the user to land 🛬. Also, the parameter values vary based on the nature of the data we are trying to land  🛬.
  • CategoryId = personalData , jobData , compensation
  • cardId = personalInfo, jobInfo , oneTimePayment
  • username= John123

 

  1. Separators – There are different separators based on the nature of the parameters we are trying to separate it, they are ( “/” , “&” , “?” , “#”)

 

  • /  - Character serves as a path separator within the URL, much like it does in standard web URLs. Separates the main deep link path ( /sf/) from the specific action or page you're targeting.

  • ? – Act as the separator between the base URL and parameters (To recall parameters please refer to the point no 3)

 

  • & -  Acts as a separator between different parameters or key-value pairs within a URL.

 

  • # - Act as an anchor in the URL, which helps to direct the user to a specific section or element within the page. 

Configured Deeplink :

– Straight Navigation to Personal Data

 

Further granular navigation to Personal Information Detail Page.

Further, you can flip (or) change the values as per your navigation requirements.

 

Spoiler
My other Articles Links:
Read audit report configuration - Read Audit Report Configuration - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Can in-cell charts in SAC tables be enable/disabled u...
entry.content:
Hi,

There is no direct API currently to enable or disable in-cell charts via script. However, in-cell charts are tied to a measure. Therefore, you can clone your measure (just create a calculated measure directly referencing the other measure) and then only enable in-cell charts for one of them. Then in your script, you can simply toggle between the two measures by setting a filter on the measures dimension.

For example, simple code for a switch widget might look like:

if (this.isOn()) {
	Table_1.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter(Alias.MeasureDimension,"Amount");
} else {
	Table_1.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter(Alias.MeasureDimension,"11533541-0732-4718-3719-721854309968");
}

This way you still only have one table widget, but the user just sees the in-cell charts toggle on or off.

Cheers, Ivan.

entry.title: Re: Role assignment confusion
entry.content:
I understand that you are getting to choose default identity provider or IAS. This is because the default identity provider and IAS are setup as active and allowed for users login, in the trust settings in the sub account where build workzone runs.

What I'm asking is, in IAS, is it configured to act as the idp (which means IAS is doing the user authentication) or is IAS configured to forward the request to your corporate identify provider (Azure for example) for authentication. The reason I ask this is because there has to be a parameter mapping between idp and build workzone so it know which user is assigned to which group and that group is assigned to roles in BTP.

entry.title: Flexible workflow Supplier invoices for migrated POs
entry.content:
Hi,

Our customer is running S4 Cloud Public Edition and had go-live 1st of June. Old POs has been migrated into S4 by Migration Cocpit and XML-files. Flexible workflow has been arranged in the system for supplier invoices for both PO-invoices and Non-PO-invoices. Workflow to approve PO-invoices has been created where it should be based on the requestor in the PR, which is fine when we have been creating PR and converted into a PO.

But for the POs that has been migrated there isn’t any PR connected, when we register the supplier invoice the Workflow selects the creator of the Purchase Order as the one to approve. We’ve been trying to add the business user ID within the PO manually, but the system still doesn’t understand that that person is the one to approve the supplier invoice.

When comparing the different POs (Migrated vs PR-PO in the system) we can see that in the PR-PO in the system there is one extra tab for Flexible Workflow and on the line-item level we’ve information related to PR, requestor.

We’r thinking of ways to handle this, has anyone any creative solution? This will be a one-time issue to solve but there are a lot of migrated POs that will need to be handled.

Kind Regards, Sanne

entry.title: SALV Report with Checkbox Selection and Designation Based Toolbar Buttons in SAP ABAP
entry.content:
Introduction 

In many SAP reporting scenarios, there is a need to control available user actions dynamically, rather than defining all functions statically upfront. Instead of displaying a fixed set of toolbar buttons in an SALV report, we can adjust them at runtime based on various conditions — such as user designation (e.g., a Manager may need buttons like "Approve" and "Reject", while a Sales Executive may only need a "Detail View" button), record status (e.g., if a sales order is already approved, the "Approve" button should be hidden or disabled ), or business logic(eg.,showing a "Reschedule" button only if the delivery date is today). 

This blog demonstrates how to: 

  • Display sales order data in a SALV report, 
  • Add checkbox selection for line-level actions, 
  • Implement custom toolbar buttons (Approve, Reject, Detail View ), 
  • Dynamically control toolbar buttons using Designation (via radio buttons). 

Business Requirement 

We are writing an SALV report that displays Sales Order details. Depending on the designation selected by the user, the SALV should display different function buttons: 

Designation 

Visible Buttons 

Manager 

Approve, Reject 

Sales Executive 

Detail View 

Additionally, users can select specific records using checkboxes, and based on button clicks, perform actions like updating statuses and navigating to VA03 (Display Sales Order). 

Step-by-Step Implementation  

Define Data Structures 

Define a custom structure for sales order data with an additional CHECK field for checkbox selection:  
Define Internal tables and Variables 

 

*-- Define a structure for Sales Order data 
 TYPES : BEGIN OF ty_sales, 
           vbeln  TYPE vbeln_va, "sales document 
           erdat  TYPE erdat,    "Created On 
           vkorg  TYPE vkorg,    "Sales Organization 
           kunnr  TYPE kunnr,    "customer 
           netwr  TYPE netwr_ak, "Net value 
           status TYPE zch_de_status,   "custom field Status 
           check  TYPE char1,    "For Checkbox selection 
         END OF ty_sales.  

*-- Declare required internal tables and variables 
 DATA : lv_vbeln   TYPE vbak-vbeln, 
        lt_vbak    TYPE TABLE OF ty_sales, 
        lo_salv    TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table, 
        lo_toolbar TYPE REF TO cl_salv_functions_list, 
        lo_events  TYPE REF TO cl_salv_events_table, 
        lo_columns TYPE REF TO cl_salv_columns, 
        lo_column  TYPE REF TO cl_salv_column_list, 
        lo_column1 TYPE REF TO cl_salv_column.

Create Designation-Based Selection Screen 

Here, p_r1 = Manager and p_r2 = Sales Executive. 

*-- Selection screen with sales document and designation selection 
 SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b1 WITH FRAME . 
  SELECT-OPTIONS : s_vbeln FOR lv_vbeln. 
   PARAMETERS : p_r1 TYPE c RADIOBUTTON GROUP r1, 
                p_r2 TYPE c RADIOBUTTON GROUP r1. 
 SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b1.

Fetch Sales Order Data Display using SALV Factory method 
The SALV object is created using the factory method and filled with data from the custom table zch_dt_vbak 

START-OF-SELECTION. 
   IF lt_vbak  IS INITIAL. 
     SELECT FROM zch_dt_vbak FIELDS vbeln, 
                               erdat, 
                               vkorg, 
                               kunnr, 
                               netwr, 
                               status 
     WHERE vbeln IN @s_vbeln 
     INTO TABLE _vbak. 
   ENDIF. 

  DATA: lx_msg TYPE REF TO cx_salv_msg. 
   TRY. 
       cl_salv_table=>factory( 
         IMPORTING 
           r_salv_table   =   lo_alv         
         CHANGING 
           t_table        = lt_vbak 
       ). 
     CATCH cx_salv_msg. " ALV: General Error Class with Message 
   ENDTRY.

Add Checkbox for selecting the rows 

  • To get the selectable (editable) checkbox, we need to get the specific column from the column object.  
  • After this, we need to set the cell type as IF_SALV_C_CELL_TYPE=>CHECKBOX_HOTSPOT by using the method SET_CELL_TYPE.  
  • To update the values in the checkbox, we need to handle the event LINK_CLICK. 
  •  This event LINK_CLICK is triggered when we click on the hotspot enabled checkbox. 
  •  In the event handler method, we need to change the value of the checkbox field and call the REFRESH method to refresh the value on the ALV. 
lo_salv->get_columns( RECEIVING value = lo_columns ). 
  lo_columns->set_optimize('X'). 
   TRY. 
       lo_column ?= lo_columns->get_column( 'CHECK' ). 
       lo_column->set_cell_type( if_salv_c_cell_type=>checkbox_hotspot ). 
       lo_column->set_output_length( 10 ). 
     CATCH cx_salv_not_found. 
   ENDTRY.

Set Column Position and Header (Optional) 

For better UI, position the checkbox as the first column by using SET_COLUMN_POSITION and rename the field by using SET_LONG_TEXT. 

lo_columns->set_column_position( columnname = 'CHECK' position = 1 ). 
   TRY. 
       CALL METHOD lo_columns->get_column 
         EXPORTING 
           columnname = 'CHECK' 
         RECEIVING 
           value      = lo_column1. 
       lo_column1->set_long_text( value = 'Select' ). 
   CATCH cx_salv_not_found. 
   ENDTRY.

Event Handler methods for handling toolbar and checkbox 

CLASS lcl_event DEFINITION. 
   PUBLIC SECTION. 
     METHODS handler FOR EVENT added_function OF cl_salv_events_table. 
     METHODS handle_click FOR EVENT link_click OF cl_salv_events_table        IMPORTING row. 
ENDCLASS.

Implementation for ‘handle_click’ method 

METHOD handle_click. 
     READ TABLE lt_vbak ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_vbak>) INDEX row. 
     IF <ls_vbak>-check IS INITIAL. 
       <ls_vbak>-check = 'X'. 
     ELSE. 
       CLEAR <ls_vbak>-check. 
     ENDIF. 
     lo_salv->refresh( ). 
   ENDMETHOD.

In SALV, we can add custom toolbar buttons using the SET_SCREEN_STATUS method and maintain the function codes and buttons using transaction SE41. 

IF p_r1 = 'X'. 
     CALL METHOD lo_salv->set_screen_status 
       EXPORTING 
         report   = sy-repid 
         pfstatus = 'TOOLBAR'. 
   ELSE. 
     CALL METHOD lo_salv->set_screen_status 
       EXPORTING 
         report   = sy-repid 
         pfstatus = 'TOOLBAR2'. 
   ENDIF.

 

Handle User Actions Based on Selected Checkbox and Button  

 METHOD handler.
    DATA : lv_count TYPE i,
           r_ucomm  TYPE sy-ucomm.

    LOOP AT lt_vbak ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_vbak>) WHERE check = 'X' .
      CASE sy-ucomm.
        WHEN 'APPROVE'.
          <fs_vbak>-status = 'Approved'.
        WHEN 'REJECT'.
          <fs_vbak>-status = 'Rejected'.
        WHEN 'DET_VIEW'.
          SET PARAMETER ID 'AUN' FIELD <fs_vbak>-vbeln.
          CALL TRANSACTION 'VA03' AND SKIP FIRST SCREEN.
      ENDCASE.
      lv_count += 1.
    ENDLOOP.
    r_ucomm = sy-ucomm.
    IF r_ucomm = '&SAVE'.
      MODIFY zch_dt_vbak FROM TABLE lt_vbak.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        MESSAGE 'Changes saved successfully.' TYPE 'S'.
      ELSE.
        MESSAGE 'Error while saving changes to the database.' TYPE 'E'.
      ENDIF.
    ELSEIF lv_count = 0 AND p_r1 = 'X'.
      MESSAGE 'Please select at least one Sales Order.' TYPE 'I'.
    ENDIF.
    lo_salv->refresh( ).

Full Implementation Code 

REPORT zch_rp_salv_report.
*-- Define a structure for Sales Order data
TYPES : BEGIN OF ty_sales,
          vbeln  TYPE vbeln_va, "sales document
          erdat  TYPE erdat,    "Created On
          vkorg  TYPE vkorg,    "Sales Organization
          kunnr  TYPE kunnr,    "customer
          netwr  TYPE netwr_ak, "Net value
          status TYPE zch_de_status,   "custom field Status
          check  TYPE char1,    "For Checkbox selection
        END OF ty_sales.

*-- Declare required internal tables and variables
DATA : lv_vbeln   TYPE vbak-vbeln,
       lt_vbak    TYPE TABLE OF ty_sales,
       lo_salv    TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table,
       lo_toolbar TYPE REF TO cl_salv_functions_list,
       lo_events  TYPE REF TO cl_salv_events_table,
       lo_columns TYPE REF TO cl_salv_columns,
       lo_column  TYPE REF TO cl_salv_column_list,
       lo_column1 TYPE REF TO cl_salv_column.

*-- Selection screen with sales document and role selection
SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b1 WITH FRAME .
  SELECT-OPTIONS : s_vbeln FOR lv_vbeln.
  PARAMETERS : p_r1 TYPE c RADIOBUTTON GROUP r1,
               p_r2 TYPE c RADIOBUTTON GROUP r1.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b1.

CLASS lcl_event DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS handler FOR EVENT added_function OF cl_salv_events_table.
    METHODS handle_click FOR EVENT link_click OF cl_salv_events_table IMPORTING row.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS lcl_event IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD handler.
    DATA : lv_count TYPE i,
           r_ucomm  TYPE sy-ucomm.

    LOOP AT lt_vbak ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_vbak>) WHERE check = 'X' .
      CASE sy-ucomm.
        WHEN 'APPROVE'.
          <fs_vbak>-status = 'Approved'.
        WHEN 'REJECT'.
          <fs_vbak>-status = 'Rejected'.
        WHEN 'DET_VIEW'.
          SET PARAMETER ID 'AUN' FIELD <fs_vbak>-vbeln.
          CALL TRANSACTION 'VA03' AND SKIP FIRST SCREEN.
      ENDCASE.
      lv_count += 1.
    ENDLOOP.
    r_ucomm = sy-ucomm.
    IF r_ucomm = '&SAVE'.
      MODIFY zch_dt_vbak FROM TABLE lt_vbak.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        MESSAGE 'Changes saved successfully.' TYPE 'S'.
      ELSE.
        MESSAGE 'Error while saving changes to the database.' TYPE 'E'.
      ENDIF.
    ELSEIF lv_count = 0 AND p_r1 = 'X'.
      MESSAGE 'Please select at least one Sales Order.' TYPE 'I'.
    ENDIF.
    lo_salv->refresh( ).
  ENDMETHOD.
  METHOD handle_click.
    READ TABLE lt_vbak ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_vbak>) INDEX row.
    IF <ls_vbak>-check IS INITIAL.
      <ls_vbak>-check = 'X'.
    ELSE.
      CLEAR <ls_vbak>-check.
    ENDIF.
    lo_salv->refresh( ).
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  IF lt_vbak  IS INITIAL.
    SELECT FROM zch_dt_vbak FIELDS vbeln,
                              erdat,
                              vkorg,
                              kunnr,
                              netwr,
                              status
    WHERE vbeln IN @s_vbeln
    INTO TABLE _vbak.
  ENDIF.

  DATA: lx_msg TYPE REF TO cx_salv_msg.
  TRY.
      cl_salv_table=>factory(
        IMPORTING
          r_salv_table   =   lo_salv  " Basis Class Simple ALV Tables
        CHANGING
          t_table        = lt_vbak
      ).
    CATCH cx_salv_msg. " ALV: General Error Class with Message
  ENDTRY.

  lo_salv->get_columns( RECEIVING value = lo_columns ).

  lo_columns->set_optimize('X').
  TRY.
      lo_column ?= lo_columns->get_column( 'CHECK' ).
      lo_column->set_cell_type( if_salv_c_cell_type=>checkbox_hotspot ).
      lo_column->set_output_length( 10 ).
    CATCH cx_salv_not_found.
  ENDTRY.

  lo_columns->set_column_position( columnname = 'CHECK' position = 1 ).
  TRY.
      CALL METHOD lo_columns->get_column
        EXPORTING
          columnname = 'CHECK'
        RECEIVING
          value      = lo_column1.
      lo_column1->set_long_text( value = 'Select' ).
    CATCH cx_salv_not_found.
  ENDTRY.

  IF p_r1 = 'X'.
    CALL METHOD lo_salv->set_screen_status
      EXPORTING
        report   = sy-repid
        pfstatus = 'TOOLBAR'.
  ELSE.
    CALL METHOD lo_salv->set_screen_status
      EXPORTING
        report   = sy-repid
        pfstatus = 'TOOLBAR2'.
  ENDIF.
 CALL METHOD lo_salv->get_event
    RECEIVING
      value = lo_events.

  DATA(lo_handler) = NEW lcl_event( ).
  DATA(lo_handle_click) = NEW lcl_event( ).
  SET HANDLER lo_handler->handler FOR lo_events.
  SET HANDLER lo_handle_click->handle_click FOR lo_events.

  CALL METHOD lo_salv->display( ).

Output : 

When I select Manager, then ‘Approve’ and ‘Reject’ Button will appear 

When user Selects the sales order using checkbox and click on Approve or Reject, the Status field will be updated and when clicked on SAVE it saves in the database table along with status. 

Database: 

If the user selects the Sales Executive radio button, then only the Detail view button will be visible when the user can navigate to VA03 getting detail info of that sales order. 

 

I selected sales order 20, so when user clicks on Detail View, it displays detail information of that specific record. 

Conclusion: 
This approach of integrating checkbox selection with designation-based dynamic toolbar control in SALV reports significantly improves both user experience and operational accuracy. By tailoring available actions based on user designation, we not only reduce clutter and potential errors but also promote better decision-making aligned with business roles. 

Leveraging the flexibility of OOABAP and SALV, this solution modernizes classic ALV interactions in a clean, maintainable way. 

I hope this blog helps you implement similar logic in your own SALV reports.

entry.title: Quickly Generate and Deploy SAP Fiori Apps from ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse
entry.content:
Last year, SAP Fiori tools added a closer integration with ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse and enabled seamless navigation from ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse to SAP Fiori tools in both SAP Business Application Studio and Visual Studio Code. This enabled you to launch your configured external IDE environment and automatically start the SAP Fiori Application generator directly from the service binding editor in ADT. 

To increase your development productivity even more, this tool integration was now reused for the new Quick Fiori Application generator to create and deploy simple SAP Fiori applications. Like with SAP Fiori Tools in SAP Business Application Studio/Visual Studio Code, you as a developer are now able to generate an application based on SAP Fiori elements annotations and deploy the SAP Fiori UI effortlessly with only few clicks from the service binding editor in ADT. 

Want to know how?

  1. As a developer, create an OData UI Service based on the business object interface or CDS view suitable for UI consumption. OData UI services can be created in ABAP Development Tools for eclipse either manually by creating an OData UI service binding or by utilizing the Generate ABAP Repository Objects wizard. See Creating Service Binding and Generating ABAP Repository Objects.

  2. When creating the OData UI service in ABAP development tools for Eclipse, you must publish the corresponding service binding. For more information, see Using Service Binding Editor for OData V4 Service.

  3. In the service binding editor, select the entity set and choose Create Fiori App... 

     

  4. You now see two creation modes with which you can create SAP Fiori apps and deploy them to SAP BTP ABAP environment. Choose Create SAP Fiori app with Quick Fiori Application generator in ADT and choose Next.

     

  5. Enter the package name and configure the parameter values ensuring the repository name is unique and within 15 characters including namespace. Like in SAP Business Application Studio, the repository name corresponds to the name of the BSP application that gets generated post deployment. 

     

  6. Choose a transport request and choose Finish. The ABAP repository objects will be generated in the background, and you get a popup message once the objects are generated successfully. Choose Open to open or refresh the service binding.

  7. The BSP application and Fiori Launchpad App Descriptor item are generated in the chosen package along with two SICF objects. You now have a basic deployed Fiori app.

  8. In the service binding editor, you now see a clickable Fiori App URL. 


    Note, that the URL won't work for cloud systems immediately.     To consume services in an SAP Fiori app, you need to complete the following step.

  9. To allow business users to access the Fiori app, you must create an IAM app and link it to a business catalog. After that, your administrator can add your app to SAP Fiori Launchpad.
    Important: To make the app visible in the Fiori Launchpad in the end, the Fiori Launchpad App Descriptor Item that was generated after deployment needs to be added to the corresponding field in the IAM App. 


    This should automatically populate the OData service under the Services tab: 

That’s it! Easy, right? 

In the project explorer beneath your package, you now see the BSP application, the Launchpad App Descriptor item and the SICF objects along with the IAM app and the business catalog. 

To summarize, the new Quick Fiori Application generator – available as of SAP BTP ABAP environment 2505 and ABAP development tools for Eclipse client version 3.50 – provides you the ability to quickly generate a productive Fiori app for simple use case (CRUD on tabular data) and configure Fiori Launchpad settings all in one without a tool switch. 

However, the Quick Fiori App generator only supports list report floorplan-based Fiori apps. Besides that, UI development capabilities are limited to backend Fiori elements annotations. For any UI changes, or if you want to delete, redeploy or undeploy the application, you must generate a Fiori project from the deployed application subsequently. As mentioned in the beginning of this blog post, the creation of a Fiori project is also supported via the Create Fiori App… button in the Service Binding editor, from where you then can take over the project to SAP Business Application Studio/Visual Studio Code to perform further actions. Your Quick Fiori app will then be overwritten during deployment. 

We think, the Quick Fiori App generator is a powerful feature that enhances your productivity and enables you as a developer to deliver applications more rapidly and effectively. What do you think of this feature? Let us know in the comments!

entry.title: Re: ABAP CDS Cheat Sheet: Amounts and Quantities in ABAP CDS
entry.content:
Hello Andrea,

we want to extract data from a base table, we use the standard CDS View as source.

If we build a CDS view entity on top of this CDS view,

and we add in projection list some amount fields that have currency defined in another base table,

we have an error "Reference Fields in this entity must be defined locally". (even using 

@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: false)

Do not solve the issue.

This is a big limitation as we do not want to define a join or association as the data extraction will create triggers in both tables.

How can we bypass this limitation?

we find that CDS view allow external element referencing for example

@Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 't001.waers'

While CDS View entity do not offer this feature.

 

Are there some workaround?

 

Best Regards,

Alberto

entry.title: Re: Onboarding - Learning integration for Pre-day1 New Hires
entry.content:
Hi,

When I have completed this integration, the workaround we used for the assignment profiles was to do a double negative rule... Since the onboardee users come in with no employee information, such as organization, department, job code, etc., we made the AP rule, Department = Is Empty, and so on. 

Another way that should be possible, but I have not tested myself, is you can add an additional static column in the Integration Center Job for one of the standard fields (ex; Department) and make it a default value such as 'ONBOARDEE' and then use this value to grab the AP population.

Hope this helps!

Melanie

entry.title: Changes to the Consumption-Based, Commercial Model Balance Statement
entry.content:
Monthly Balance Statement

Get an overview of your current balance for your Enterprise Agreement 

 

Monthly Balance Statements for Consumption-Based, Commercial Model to display more accurate data in respect to Unit of Measure (UoM) effective May 2025.

 

WHAT ARE THE CHANGES TO EXPECT?

  • ❎Excluded: Charged usages metric displays (Piece) PC as Unit of Measure (UoM) across all balance statement layouts 
  • ☑️Included: Introducing two new column Sales Metric AND Block Size as Unit of Measure (UoM)

 

Spoiler
Balance Statement Layout before May 2025

 

Balance Statement (NEW) Layout effective May 2025

New Layout

WHICH CONSUMPTION-BASED, COMMERCIAL MODELS WILL BE CHANGED?

This new display will be implemented for the following consumption-based commercial model: 

  • Pay-As-You-Go for SAP 
  • SAP BTP Enterprise Agreement
  • Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement (Sunset/Existing Customers) 

 

WHERE WILL THE CHANGES OCCUR?

At the Consumption Header level. This includes if a Balance statement layout contains section to display Quota (Hybrid Contract) and(or) Free tier section, the same changes will apply also for the materials shown in this section.

Here are some examples of mock balance statements by each models:

PAYG

Spoiler
PAYG

BTPEA

Spoiler
BTPEA

CPEA (Net)

Spoiler
CPEA Net Model

Hybrid Contract

Spoiler
CPEA/PAYG + Subscription Combined

Free tier section

Spoiler
Free Tier

OTHER CONSIDERATIONS

Same change should be reflected also in the Excel Balance Statement: The template has to be updated for all scenarios, so that column "Unit of Measure" will be replaced by "Sales Metric" and eventually "Block Size".

**Note: "PC" will not be shown in the excel Balance Statement anymore. 

 

Contact Support

In case of queries concerning the Balance Statement please contact sapbalancestatement@sap.com

entry.title: Consuming data from SAP Datasphere in SAP BTP CAP Applications
entry.content:
Premise:

Often, an application developed on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) requires data from various systems for different purposes. SAP Datasphere, which unifies and integrates all SAP and non-SAP data from various sources, is an excellent resource for such business data. In a recent project with one of the leading banks in Europe, we observed that machine learning experts preferred working directly with the underlying data using Python for building and training models. However, since the SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) Model does not natively support Python, it's important to consider alternative approaches that allow integration with Python-based workflows and thus bridging the gap.

In the context of an enterprise, imagine a large business with stringent internal and external requirements for data quality, ensuring the reliability of any form of data that is processed, particularly if the documents are related to financial accounting, as these are very critical. Considering table in S/4HANA, which combines multiple financial journals into one consolidated table and holds all the financial data, has high volume and complexity that can give rise to data quality issues. These issues can be costly when discovered at later stages of accounting-related processes, posing significant challenges to the enterprise. Given the high complexity and variety in accounting scenarios, along with significant data volume across multiple business divisions, user data quality control measures must be complemented by machine-enabled solutions. Machine learning algorithms can play a crucial role in supporting responsible organizational units by detecting potential data quality issues early and assisting in their resolution. This proactive approach helps maintain data quality throughout the entire accounting process.

The accounting ledger line-item data from the S/4HANA application can be sourced into SAP Datasphere to perform anomaly detection using machine learning, ensuring no additional load on the operational S/4 system. The anomaly detection algorithm identifies potential anomalies across document line items, which are then presented to end users, such as controllers, through SAP Analytics Cloud. To assist in investigating detected anomalies, business users like controllers can be provided with the reasoning behind why the machine learning algorithm flagged the relevant items as unusual.

Such an application can be easily realised by following the detailed steps outlined in the blog, Hands-on Tutorial: Machine Learning with SAP Datasphere. The blog also includes a hands-on code repository providing snippets to get you started.

Developing this concept further, an application developed on SAP BTP can interact seamlessly with the anomaly detection outcomes in natural language. Additionally, such an application can integrate with the S/4HANA system and could automate the creation of corrective actions, ensuring complete and streamlined workflow.

Solution:

The application on BTP is built following steps detailed in the reference architecture, Retrieval Augmented Generation and Generative AI on SAP BTP and the associated mission, GenAI Mail Insights: Develop a CAP application using GenAI and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG). The mission includes associated code on GitHub, btp-cap-genai-rag.

Although the provided code isn’t an exact match, it offers all the necessary elements in the code and can be easily adjusted based on your needs, such as here for building an application to interact with anomaly detection outcomes. One key difference for our scenario is to be able to refer or integrate with the data source (tables) in SAP Datasphere within the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) framework. The following two options are available for this integration.

SAP HANA Cross Container Access:

SAP CAP,  being an opinionated framework, provides instructions on how to use databases with CAP applications. SAP HANA Cloud is the recommended and standard database for productive usage. Deploying to SAP HANA from SAP CAP is done using SAP HANA Deployment Infrastructure (HDI). The SAP HANA Deployment Infrastructure (HDI) uses containers to store design-time artifacts and the corresponding deployed run-time (catalog) objects. 

Prepare SAP Datasphere space by enabling access to HDI containers. This process is used to create a mapping between SAP Datasphere and the SAP BTP sub account, thereby enabling bi-directional access. In one direction, to access the artefacts from HDI containers associated with the corresponding SAP HANA Cloud attached to one of your SAP BTP sub accounts in the SAP Datasphere spaces. In another direction, it allows the SAP HANA Cloud underneath the SAP Datasphere to also deploy HDI containers to be used for SAP CAP applications.

In SAP Datasphere space where the anomaly detections results are made, create a technical database user to expose the artefacts within the space for consumption as source for the CAP application. This database user must have appropriate privileges and should be enabled for HDI consumption.

Now, SAP HANA cross container access can be used to access data from one schema or HDI container by another. This blog details cross container access scenarios using user-provided service on cloud foundry.

The diagram below puts all these steps into perspective.

                                                 

To summarise, the SAP CAP application deploys an HDI container (denoted as consumer in the above diagram) on the SAP HANA Cloud underneath SAP Datasphere thereby enabling cross container access with the provider schema or HDI container (denoted as provider in the above diagram) of the corresponding SAP Datasphere space by defining synonyms and a user provided service. Once this is established, the data is brought into SAP CAP by defining @cds.persistence.exists for the necessary entities in the model.

The primary strategy in this option involves utilizing SAP HANA Cloud within SAP Datasphere for deploying the HDI container in the SAP CAP application. Should this option be unsuitable due to the SAP HANA Cloud being managed by SAP, an alternative approach detailed below may be considered.

SAP HANA Virtual Tables and Cross Container Access:

SAP HANA smart data access let's you access remote data as if the data were stored in local tables in SAP HANA, without copying the data into SAP HANA. In SAP HANA, linked databases or create virtual tables are used, which point to remote tables in different remote databases, and then SQL queries can be used in SAP HANA to access these virtual tables. In this option, you will need an SAP HANA Cloud instance for the CAP application. The virtual tables using smart data access will be created here, thereby linking the remote data source/tables to the space from SAP HANA Cloud underneath the SAP Datasphere.

Like option 1, In SAP Datasphere space where the anomaly detection’s results are made, create a technical database user to expose the artefacts within the space for consumption as source for the CAP application. This database user must have appropriate privileges and should be enabled for HDI consumption.

Once the access is enabled, create necessary virtual tables as described in the initial part of the blog Step by Step guide to creating remote data in HDI.

The diagram below puts all these steps into perspective.

Now, similar to option 1, the virtual tables can be used in the HDI container managed by the SAP CAP application using user-provided service on cloud foundry. The second part of the blog referred to in the previous para also details this.

Summary:

To summarise, SAP Datasphere is utilised to load business data, such as financial accounting information from S/4 HANA, and perform anomaly detections before writing back the data. Subsequently, a second application is developed using SAP CAP on SAP BTP to access these results and provide an interface that interacts with natural language using SAP AI Core. In effect, this approach bridges the gap between leveraging SAP Datasphere for centralised data processing and anomaly detection using Python and integrating the results seamlessly into a user-facing application built with SAP CAP on BTP.
